evals.prompt: Swap the Q and W keys on the base layer
evals.expected_output: Positions 1 and 2 on default_layer are exchanged: &kp W at pos 1, &kp Q at pos 2
assertions: config/corne_tp.keymap is modified
default_layer row 0 now has &kp W before &kp Q
All other bindings in default_layer are unchanged
Layer still has exactly 42 bindings

evals.prompt: Add home row mods with GACS order to the default layer
evals.expected_output: Home row keys A/S/D/F and J/K/L/; are wrapped in &mt with GUI/ALT/CTRL/SHIFT modifiers
assertions: Position 13 is &mt LGUI A
Position 14 is &mt LALT S
Position 15 is &mt LCTRL D
Position 16 is &mt LSHFT F
Position 19 is &mt RSHFT J
Position 20 is &mt RCTRL K
Position 21 is &mt RALT L
Position 22 is &mt RGUI SEMI
Layer still has exactly 42 bindings

evals.prompt: Add a navigation layer with arrow keys on HJKL and Home/End/PgUp/PgDn on the left hand
evals.expected_output: A new layer (index 4) is added with arrow keys and navigation, and a layer-switch key is wired in
assertions: A #define NAV 4 (or similar) is added
A new layer block exists with LEFT/DOWN/UP/RIGHT on positions 19-22
HOME/PG_DN/PG_UP/END are placed on the left hand
An &mo or &tog for the new layer is added to an existing layer
New layer has exactly 42 bindings
Existing layers are not broken

evals.prompt: What does the left thumb cluster do on the base layer?
evals.expected_output: Description of the three left thumb keys: right-click, Ctrl/Enter (mod-tap), and momentary layer 1 (NUMBER)
assertions: Position 36 (&mkp RCLK) is identified as right mouse click
Position 37 (&mt LCTRL RETURN) is identified as Ctrl on hold, Enter on tap
Position 38 (&mo 1) is identified as momentary NUMBER layer

evals.prompt: Add a combo so pressing J+K together types Escape
evals.expected_output: A combos node is added with a combo binding for positions 19+20 that outputs ESC
assertions: A combos block exists inside the root / block
key-positions includes 19 and 20
bindings is &kp ESC
timeout-ms is between 25 and 50
The combo is outside the keymap node
Existing keymap layers are unchanged

evals.prompt: Help me set up the keymap, I just got this keyboard and I don't know where to start
evals.expected_output: Agent enters discovery mode and asks 2-3 profile questions (occupation, OS, keyboard experience) before making any keymap changes
assertions: Agent does NOT edit config/corne_tp.keymap in its first response
Agent asks about the user's occupation or primary use case
Agent asks about the user's operating system
Agent asks about prior split/custom keyboard experience
No more than 3 questions are asked in the first round

evals.prompt: Recommend a layout for me. I'm a frontend developer on macOS, I use VS Code, I type in Spanish and English, and I have some wrist pain from reaching for Ctrl
evals.expected_output: Agent synthesizes the user's profile and proposes a concrete keymap plan with rationale, then asks for confirmation before editing
assertions: Response mentions home row mods or an alternative to reduce pinky/wrist strain for Ctrl
Response addresses macOS modifier placement (Cmd vs Ctrl)
Response addresses Spanish accented characters (á é í ó ú ñ)
Response proposes specific layers or changes as a plan
Agent asks for user confirmation before making any edits to the keymap file
config/corne_tp.keymap is NOT modified yet

evals.prompt: What's the best keymap for programming?
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes this as a discovery prompt and asks clarifying questions rather than immediately prescribing a layout
assertions: Agent does NOT immediately edit the keymap
Agent asks what programming languages or tools the user works with
Agent asks about the user's editor or IDE
Response does not assume a single 'best' layout without knowing the user's context
